title: Macroeconomics and Game Theory — SuperBEST v3 Cost Analysis

variants.label: α is a live (variable) parameter
variants.note: sub(1,α)=2 because α is live; two pow calls at 3 each; two mul calls at 2 each.

variants.label: α is a fixed constant (1−α also constant)
variants.note: Both exponents are free constants; no sub node needed.

variants.label: s, A, δ, α all live
s, A, δ fixed; α live
variants.note: sA/δ collapses to a free constant.

variants.label: s, A, δ, α all fixed constants
variants.note: Entire exponent and base are free constants.

equations.name: Phillips Curve
equations.note: Two sub nodes and one mul; purely linear so no exp/pow needed.

variants.label: Exact form — solve for i
variants.note: Two add nodes, one mul, one sub to strip the +1.

equations.name: Nash Equilibrium Expected Utility (2-outcome)
equations.note: Mixture model with complementary probability; one add for final sum.

equations.name: Logit Choice Probability (N options — softmax)

breakdown_per_N.note_denominator_reuse: Denominator is shared; numerator term already counted in denominator sum.

equations.note: O(N) cost. Denominator dominates. Identical structure to softmax in neural networks.

equations.name: Gini Coefficient from Lorenz Curve L(p) = p^(1+α)

variants.label: Closed-form analytic evaluation
variants.note: Integral ∫₀¹ p^(1+α)dp = 1/(2+α) solved analytically; result is G = α/(2+α).

variants.label: Numerical integration — per quadrature step
variants.note: Each step evaluates p^(1+α); expensive if α is live at each step.

equations.name: Compounding Inflation Adjustment
equations.note: n may be a free constant (years). One add, one pow, one div.

equations.name: Present Value of Perpetuity
equations.note: Single div node — cheapest formula in the session. Gordon growth model PV = D/(r−g) costs sub(r,g)=2 + div=1 = 3n.

summary_table.name: Solow Steady State k*
summary_table.variant_note: all live; 7n if only α live; 3n if all fixed

key_findings: PV=C/r is the absolute cheapest at 1 node (single div).
Phillips curve and Gini (analytic) tie at 6 nodes — both linear-ish after closed-form resolution.
Fisher (approx) and Solow (all-fixed) collapse to 3 nodes each.
Logit/softmax scales as 5N — same cost structure as neural network softmax.
Cobb-Douglas is the most expensive at 12n (α live); constants reduce it to 10n.
Solow steady-state spans 3–10n depending on how many parameters are fixed vs live.
sub(1,α) costs 2n only when α is a live variable; if α is a fixed constant, 1−α is also a free constant.
